message:
wa-nu4.3.9.9: docs smoke tests for quickstart commands

Add 12 docs smoke tests (crates/wa/tests/docs_smoke.rs) that validate
quickstart commands referenced in docs remain executable:

- wa --help: exits 0, contains usage info and subcommand list
- wa --version: exits 0, shows version
- wa version --full: no panic, shows build metadata
- wa doctor --json: no panic, outputs valid JSON when successful
- wa doctor (plain): no panic
- wa setup --dry-run: no panic, doesn't modify files
- wa robot quick-start: exits 0, outputs valid JSON
- wa robot (default): exits 0 (defaults to quick-start)
- wa export --help: exits 0, lists export kinds
- Unknown subcommand: exits non-zero
- Summary generator: captures pass/fail + durations as artifact

Tests run in isolated temp workspace (WA_WORKSPACE env var) with fake
WezTerm CLI path to prevent real terminal interaction. Artifacts
captured via eprintln for CI debugging.

Uses assert_cmd + predicates (already in wa dev-deps).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-13y1","title":"Config validation + wa notify test","description":"## What
Extend notifications config and add a CLI test command.

## Why
Misconfigured channels should fail fast with actionable errors and users need a safe connectivity check.

## How
- Extend existing [notifications] config with channel definitions
- CLI: `wa notify test --channel <name>` returns structured success/failure
- Reuse NotificationGate for filters and cooldown

## Success Criteria
- Invalid configs produce clear error messages
- Test command validates redaction and endpoint reachability","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentCanyon","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:08:19.633409931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T09:18:11.609739890Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T09:18:11.609661384Z","close_reason":"Added notifications config validation and new 'wa notify test --channel' with gate/redaction/delivery checks; added tests; ran fmt/check/clippy/test.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13y1","depends_on_id":"bd-2uyz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-13y1","depends_on_id":"bd-ugaj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"bd-13y1","author":"SilentCanyon","text":"Implemented notifications config validation (severity/agent types, webhook name/URL/duplicates) and added  command with gate+redaction+delivery checks plus CLI parse test. Ran cargo fmt/check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings, cargo test.","created_at":"2026-02-04T09:17:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-14da","title":"CLI: wa secrets scan/report","description":"## What
Add CLI commands to run scans and view reports.

## Why
Operators need a simple workflow to verify secret hygiene.

## How
- `wa secrets scan` runs scan and stores report
- `wa secrets report` prints counts and redacted locations

## Success Criteria
- Output is fully redacted and includes counts by pattern","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:30.346782578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:27:56.247328688Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14da","depends_on_id":"bd-2pii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-14da","depends_on_id":"bd-2yoy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-14da","depends_on_id":"bd-5wge","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-15ds","title":"[EPIC] Local RPC socket for robot/MCP (auth + caps)","description":"## Background
Robot/MCP APIs are powerful; local tools need a secure and ergonomic way to connect without parsing CLI output.

## Goals
- Provide a local IPC server over Unix domain socket / named pipe
- Authenticate with short-lived tokens and capability scopes
- Keep parity with robot/MCP schemas

## Non-Goals
- Remote network exposure (handled by distributed mode)

## Considerations
- Must be disabled by default
- Requires strict redaction and audit logging

## Success Criteria
- Local client can call core robot APIs over IPC
- Token and scope checks enforced
- Unit + e2e tests for auth and schema parity","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:13.180280826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:13.180280826Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.6.2","title":"Implement read-only endpoints: /panes /events /search (shared query layer)","description":"# Task: Implement read-only web endpoints

## Goal
Expose minimal read-only endpoints for dashboards, while preserving wa’s safety and schema-stability guarantees.

## Endpoints (v0)
- `GET /health`
- `GET /panes`
- `GET /events?unhandled=1&pane_id=...&limit=...`
- `GET /search?q=...&pane_id=...&since=...&limit=...`

## Design constraints
- Reuse the same query/model code as CLI/robot.
  - The web server should not reimplement data access.
- Responses are JSON with stable schemas and stable ordering.
- Apply the same redaction pipeline as robot/audit/export.

## Pagination & limits
- All list endpoints must be bounded.
  - enforce default and max `limit`
  - provide stable ordering (e.g., newest-first for events)

## Error handling
- Return structured JSON errors with stable error codes.
- Never leak internal DB paths or sensitive config in errors.

## Testing
- Covered by the web server tests bead (`wa-nu4.3.6.4`):
  - schema parseability
  - redaction
  - bind defaults

## Acceptance Criteria
- Endpoints return data when watcher is running.
- Responses remain schema-stable and redacted.
- List endpoints enforce sane defaults and max limits.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.6.3","title":"Web server hardening: localhost-only default, explicit bind, no secret leakage","description":"# Task: Web server hardening (safe-by-default)

## Goal
Ensure the optional `wa web` server is safe-by-default and hard to misuse.

## Threat model
Even “read-only” endpoints can leak:
- terminal transcripts (may contain secrets)
- tokens embedded in URLs
- hostnames, paths, and other sensitive metadata

So the web server must inherit wa’s redaction and safety conventions.

## Requirements
- **Bind defaults**
  - default bind: `127.0.0.1`
  - require an explicit, scary flag to bind publicly (proposed): `--dangerous-bind-any`
  - when binding non-localhost, print a prominent warning

- **No secret leakage**
  - all responses must be redacted using the same redaction pipeline as robot/audit/export
  - never include raw segment bytes by default

- **Hard limits**
  - request size limits
  - sensible default pagination limits

- **Surface area**
  - only GET endpoints in v0
  - no file-serving
  - no CORS-by-default (unless explicitly enabled)

## Testing strategy
- `cargo test --features web` includes cases that assert:
  - localhost bind is the default
  - public bind requires `--dangerous-bind-any`
  - redaction is applied to responses for any field that can contain secrets

## Acceptance Criteria
- A naive `wa web` run is not remotely reachable by default.
- Redaction is applied consistently (web == robot == audit == export).

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.6.4","title":"Web server tests: /health + basic endpoint contract (feature web)","description":"# Task: Web server tests (feature web)

## Goal
Prevent the optional web server from silently rotting.

The web server is not on the critical path, but when enabled it must be reliable, safe, and schema-stable.

## Test scope
- Runs under `cargo test --features web`.
- Fully offline (no real WezTerm required).

## Required test cases
- Lifecycle:
  - start server on `--port 0`
  - hit `/health`
  - shut down cleanly

- Contract:
  - `/health` schema is parseable and stable
  - `/panes`, `/events`, `/search` responses are:
    - valid JSON
    - stable ordering where applicable
    - redacted (no raw secrets)

- Safety:
  - default bind is localhost
  - public bind requires explicit `--dangerous-bind-any`

## Observability in tests
- Capture request logs/spans and assert a few key fields exist (method/path/status/latency).
- Assert that sensitive values do not appear in logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo test --features web` includes at least:
  - `/health` responds
  - endpoint response bodies are schema-parseable
  - redaction + bind-default invariants are enforced


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Add a negative test that attempts `--dangerous-bind-any` without explicit confirmation (if applicable) and ensure it is rejected.
  - Add an assertion that there are no write/mutation endpoints registered.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.7","title":"[EPIC] Optional TUI (charmed_rust): pane picker + event feed + transcript viewer","description":"# [EPIC] Interactive TUI Dashboard

## Mission
Transform wa from an invisible background daemon into a **visible control center** with a real-time dashboard showing panes, events, workflows, and health metrics.

## Why This Is Transformative
Terminal automation is inherently opaque. Users cannot see what wa is doing. The TUI changes this fundamentally:

**Before TUI:**
- "I hope it is working"
- "I need to check logs to see what happened"
- "I do not know if the workflow succeeded"

**After TUI:**
- "I can watch everything happening in real-time"
- "I see the workflow progressing step by step"
- "I know immediately when something needs attention"

## TUI Layout Vision
```
┌─ WA Dashboard ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Panes (4 observed, 1 ignored)                                        │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ● Pane 1 [codex]     Idle ▪ PromptActive    │ Last: 2m ago          │
│ ● Pane 3 [claude]    Running ▪ CommandActive │ Last: 5s ago          │
│ ○ Pane 5 [local]     Ignored (exclude rule) │                        │
│ ● Pane 7 [gemini]    Idle ▪ PromptActive    │ Last: 30s ago         │
│ ⚠ Pane 9 [codex]     UsageLimitReached      │ Last: 1s ago          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Events (recent)                                              [e] more │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 14:32:01 ⚠ codex.usage_limit_reached    Pane 9  → workflow started  │
│ 14:31:45 ○ session.compaction           Pane 3  → handled           │
│ 14:30:12 ○ codex.session_summary        Pane 1  → no workflow       │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Workflows                                                            │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ▶ handle_usage_limits (Pane 9)  Step 3/7: Selecting account...      │
│   [████████░░░░░░░░░░░░] 42%                                         │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Health: ● OK  │ Queue: 0  │ Lag: 12ms  │ DB: 1.2GB                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
[q]uit [p]anes [e]vents [w]orkflows [t]imeline [s]earch [h]elp
```

## Core Views

### 1. Panes Panel
- List all panes with state indicators
- Color-coded status (green=healthy, yellow=warning, red=error)
- Filter: observed/ignored, by agent type
- Select to inspect: show recent output tail, events, actions

### 2. Events Feed
- Real-time event stream
- Severity coloring (info/warning/error)
- Filter: unhandled, by type, by pane
- Select to view details and quick-fix suggestions

### 3. Workflow Progress
- Active workflows with step-by-step progress
- Progress bars and estimated completion
- Expand to see step logs
- Cancel button for stuck workflows

### 4. Health Metrics
- Overall system status
- Queue depths and lag
- DB size and write status
- Circuit breaker states

### 5. Timeline View
- Interactive scrollable timeline
- Cross-pane event correlation
- Zoom in/out on time ranges

### 6. Search/Transcript View
- FTS query interface
- Snippet display with highlighting
- Navigate to context around matches

## Keyboard Navigation
```
Tab / Shift-Tab : Move between panels
Arrow keys      : Navigate within panel
Enter           : Select/expand item
Escape          : Back / collapse
q               : Quit
?               : Help
```

## Implementation

### Framework
Use `ratatui` (successor to tui-rs) with `crossterm` backend:
- Cross-platform (macOS, Linux, Windows)
- Full Unicode support
- Mouse support (optional)

### Architecture
```
wa tui
  ├── App (main loop)
  │     ├── EventBus subscriber (real-time updates)
  │     └── Query client (same as CLI)
  ├── Widgets
  │     ├── PanesPanel
  │     ├── EventsPanel
  │     ├── WorkflowsPanel
  │     ├── HealthPanel
  │     ├── TimelineView
  │     └── SearchView
  └── State management (Redux-like)
```

### Feature Flag
`--features tui` to keep binary size down for headless deployments.

## Testing
- Unit tests: Widget rendering with mock data
- State tests: Verify state transitions
- Integration tests: Event subscription works
- Manual tests: Keyboard navigation, responsiveness

## Success Criteria
- TUI launches with `wa tui` and shows real-time dashboard
- All panels update in real-time as events occur
- Keyboard navigation is intuitive and documented
- Performance: <50ms render time, <100MB memory
- Accessibility: Works with screen readers (basic)

## Acceptance Criteria
- TUI launches reliably behind feature flag.
- Pane picker, event feed, and transcript viewer work with live data.
- Workflow and health panels render without crashes.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.7.1","title":"TUI scaffolding (charmed_rust): feature flag + app skeleton + shared query client","description":"# Task: TUI scaffolding (charmed_rust, feature tui)

## Goal
Create an **optional** interactive UI entrypoint: `wa tui`, behind `--features tui`, that provides a foundation for panes/events/search views.

The purpose of this bead is the **app skeleton** and **data access architecture**, not the final UX polish.

## Architecture (critical: keep UI dumb)
- Implement a small TUI app framework layer (initial screen router + event loop).
- Define a `QueryClient` trait (exact name flexible) used by the TUI:
  - `list_panes()`
  - `list_events(filters)`
  - `search(query)`
- Provide a production implementation backed by existing wa query surfaces:
  - prefer calling the same internal query/model layer that powers `wa robot ...`
  - never query SQLite directly from UI widgets

This separation is what makes the TUI testable and prevents “UI spaghetti + DB coupling”.

## UX baseline (v0)
- Global keybindings:
  - `q` quit
  - `?` help
  - `r` refresh
  - `tab` / `shift+tab` switch views (or a visible nav)
- Clear empty/error states:
  - watcher not running
  - DB not reachable
  - permission/policy denied

## Logging & diagnostics
- Use the structured logging baseline (tracing spans) so TUI bugs can be debugged.
- Avoid logging transcript contents; log counts/ids and redacted summaries only.

## Testing strategy
- `cargo test --features tui` includes:
  - unit tests for the view-model/reducer (state transitions) using a fake `QueryClient`
  - snapshot tests for non-ANSI text render helpers (headers/rows formatting)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa tui` starts and shows a placeholder screen with nav + help.
- The app uses a shared query client abstraction (no direct DB calls from views).
- Basic error states render cleanly (e.g., “watcher not running”).

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.7.2","title":"TUI panes view: list panes + filter + select to inspect","description":"# Task: TUI panes view

## Goal
Provide an interactive pane picker that makes it fast to answer:
- “Which pane is my agent running in?”
- “Is it at a prompt or mid-command?”
- “Which panes have unhandled events?”

This is the TUI equivalent of `wa status` + quick drill-down.

## Data contract
Back the view via the shared query layer / robot state model:
- pane id
- domain + workspace
- title + cwd
- inferred agent type (Codex/Claude/Gemini/unknown)
- inferred pane state (PromptActive/CommandRunning/AltScreen/unknown)
- last segment/event timestamp
- unhandled event count (if available)

## UX requirements
- List view:
  - stable columns and readable truncation
  - highlights for “needs attention” (unhandled events / alt-screen / gaps)
- Filtering:
  - free-text filter on title/cwd/domain
  - quick toggles: unhandled-only, agent filter, domain filter
- Selection details:
  - show a detail panel with full fields
  - show “next best actions” hints (e.g., suggest `wa workflow …` when unhandled events exist)

## Performance constraints
- Must handle hundreds of panes without lag.
- Prefer incremental updates (refresh tick) rather than re-rendering everything per keystroke.

## Testing strategy
- Unit tests (feature `tui`) using a fake `QueryClient`:
  - filtering logic correctness
  - sort order stability
  - selection state transitions
  - large list performance sanity (no quadratic behavior in reducer)

## Acceptance Criteria
- User can filter panes and select one.
- Details panel shows basic info (title/cwd/state/last_activity/unhandled_count).
- The view remains responsive with a large synthetic pane list.
","notes":"Completed by MaroonCreek (2026-02-06): implemented panes filtering + detail panel + query enrichment. Added free-text + quick toggle filters (u/a/d), detail panel with next-action hints, stable filtered index helper, and tests for filtering/navigation/large-list stability.  {"id":"wa-nu4.3.7.3","title":"TUI events feed: recent/unhandled, severity colors, select to view details","description":"# Task: TUI events feed

## Goal
Provide an interactive event feed that makes it trivial to:
- see **what wa thinks is happening**
- quickly spot **unhandled / high-severity** events
- drill into why a rule matched (pack/rule/trace)

This is the “incident timeline” view.

## Data contract
Back the view via the same model as `wa robot events`:
- event id
- timestamp
- pane id + domain
- severity
- handled/unhandled status
- rule/pack identifiers
- short redacted summary
- optional match trace (what text matched, in redacted form)

## UX requirements
- Feed view:
  - default sort: newest-first
  - toggle: unhandled-only
  - filter by pane id / domain / event type
  - severity coloring (must degrade gracefully for non-color terminals)

- Event details panel:
  - show full structured fields
  - show rule id + pack id
  - show remediation hint / “recommended next action” if present

## Safety
- Never render raw transcript content that could include secrets.
- Always use redacted summaries/traces.

## Testing strategy
- Unit tests (feature `tui`) using fake data:
  - filter toggles work
  - severity sorting is stable
  - details panel renders expected fields
  - redaction is preserved (no accidental display of raw secrets)

## Acceptance Criteria
- User can toggle unhandled-only and immediately see unhandled critical events.
- Selecting an event shows rule/pack ids and a redacted trace/details view.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.6","title":"CI e2e job: run scripts with verbose logging, keep artifacts on failure","description":"# Task: CI e2e job

## Goal
Run end-to-end tests automatically when possible and make failures diagnosable.

## Design
- Add a CI job that:
  - installs/ensures WezTerm (if feasible) OR runs in an environment where WezTerm exists
  - runs `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all --verbose`
  - captures artifacts directory
  - uploads artifacts on failure

## Failure UX
- CI output should point directly to the artifacts.
- The e2e script should print a concise summary at the end.

## Registry
- Not a case. CI job must execute registry cases (`./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all`) and retain artifacts.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A failing e2e run produces an artifact bundle that includes wa logs + wezterm logs + exports.


## Testing
- CI self-check:
  - Add a mode (or a dedicated small case) that intentionally fails so we can verify artifact upload behavior.
  - Ensure the job prints the artifacts path/URL in the log summary.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.7","title":"CI e2e (extended): run approval allow-once scenario + keep artifacts","description":"# Task: CI extended e2e job

## Goal
Run the phase-4 "approval allow-once" E2E scenario in CI once the feature set is available.

This job specifically validates:
- RequireApproval UX payload
- wa approve command
- approval storage
- audited outcomes + redaction

## Behavior
- Execute:
  - ./scripts/e2e_test.sh --case approval-allow-once --verbose
- Always upload artifacts when failing.

## Registry
- Not a case. CI job extends the registry run with additional approval/allow-once scenarios.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A CI failure includes enough artifacts to diagnose whether the bug is in:
  - policy decision
  - approval granting
  - retry behavior
  - audit/export


## Testing
- CI self-check:
  - Ensure the job logs include the artifacts upload location.
  - Consider a “known fail” dry run to verify artifact upload and summary formatting.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.8","title":"CI setup job: run wa setup idempotency E2E (temp home, artifacts on failure)","description":"# Task: CI setup job (dotfile patching safety)

## Goal
Continuously validate that wa setup is safe and idempotent.

Dotfile patching is one of the fastest ways to lose user trust. This CI job ensures:
- dry-run does not write
- apply mode changes only the intended block
- re-running is a no-op

## Behavior
- Run the setup idempotency E2E:
  - ./scripts/e2e_setup_test.sh --all --verbose
  - or reuse ./scripts/e2e_test.sh if we unify harnesses

- Always upload artifacts on failure:
  - before/after copies
  - diffs
  - logs

## Dependencies
- Uses the E2E setup test described in wa-nu4.3.3.10.

## Registry
- Not a case. CI job focuses on setup idempotency cases from the registry.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI failures include all artifacts needed to debug the patcher step.


## Testing
- CI self-check:
  - Ensure the job asserts it is operating on a temp home directory and never touches `~`.
  - Include an intentional failure variant to verify artifact upload (diffs/before-after) works.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","title":"Docs smoke tests: quickstart commands stay executable in CI","description":"# Task: Docs smoke tests (quickstart stays executable)

## Goal
Prevent docs drift by ensuring the README/quickstart instructions remain executable and safe.

This is a user-trust feature: if the quickstart breaks, new users churn.

## Scope
- Focus on commands that docs will recommend for a fresh install:
  - `wa --help`
  - `wa --version --verbose`
  - `wa robot quick-start`
  - `wa setup --dry-run` (in a temp HOME)
  - `wa doctor` (in a minimal environment; may be expected to warn but must be actionable)

- For commands that require a real WezTerm environment, docs should point to the E2E scripts as the canonical tested path rather than embedding fragile “just run this” snippets.

## Deliverables
1) A small docs-smoke runner (CI-friendly)
- Runs in a temp HOME + temp workspace.
- Captures stdout/stderr to artifacts.
- Never modifies real user dotfiles.

2) A mapping from “docs claims” → “test coverage”
- For each quickstart step, link to either:
  - a docs-smoke command, or
  - an E2E script case (e.g., `wa-4vx.10.7`, `wa-nu4.3.3.10`).

3) CI integration
- Add a CI job/step that runs docs smoke and uploads artifacts on failure.

## Logging & artifacts (must match the E2E philosophy)
- Artifacts directory contains:
  - `env.txt` (OS, rustc, cargo, enabled features)
  - `commands.txt` (exact commands executed)
  - `stdout.txt` / `stderr.txt` per command (or per step)
  - `summary.json` (pass/fail + durations)

- Logs must never contain secrets (even if users have real configs on their machine).

## Testing
- Unit-ish tests:
  - if docs smoke is implemented as a Rust subcommand, validate output schemas and deterministic ordering.
  - if implemented as a shell script, include at least one self-test that verifies it creates artifacts and returns correct exit codes.

- CI tests:
  - docs smoke runs on every PR and fails when a quickstart command regresses.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Docs smoke runs in CI and fails when a referenced quickstart command breaks.
- Failures are diagnosable from artifacts without rerunning locally.


## Testing
- “Tests for the tests”:
  - include one intentionally-failing step under a special flag (or a dedicated CI mode) to ensure artifact capture + summaries are working.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:42:24.357386135Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:47:56.836891063Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-ci","area-docs","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:41Z","created_by":"import"}]}

- Runs in a temp HOME + temp workspace.
- Captures stdout/stderr to artifacts.
- Never modifies real user dotfiles.

2) A mapping from “docs claims” → “test coverage”
- For each quickstart step, link to either:
  - a docs-smoke command, or
  - an E2E script case (e.g., `wa-4vx.10.7`, `wa-nu4.3.3.10`).

3) CI integration
- Add a CI job/step that runs docs smoke and uploads artifacts on failure.

## Logging & artifacts (must match the E2E philosophy)
- Artifacts directory contains:
  - `env.txt` (OS, rustc, cargo, enabled features)
  - `commands.txt` (exact commands executed)
  - `stdout.txt` / `stderr.txt` per command (or per step)
  - `summary.json` (pass/fail + durations)

- Logs must never contain secrets (even if users have real configs on their machine).

## Testing
- Unit-ish tests:
  - if docs smoke is implemented as a Rust subcommand, validate output schemas and deterministic ordering.
  - if implemented as a shell script, include at least one self-test that verifies it creates artifacts and returns correct exit codes.

- CI tests:
  - docs smoke runs on every PR and fails when a quickstart command regresses.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Docs smoke runs in CI and fails when a referenced quickstart command breaks.
- Failures are diagnosable from artifacts without rerunning locally.


## Testing
- “Tests for the tests”:
  - include one intentionally-failing step under a special flag (or a dedicated CI mode) to ensure artifact capture + summaries are working.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.4","title":"[EPIC] Phase 5: Advanced — vendoring, streaming, distributed mode, coordination","description":"# Phase 5 — Advanced Features

## Objective
Push wa from “solid local orchestrator” into “fleet-grade system”:
- Selective WezTerm vendoring (feature-gated) for streaming output / lower overhead.
- Real-time output streaming (reduce polling and gaps).
- Optional distributed mode: `wa-agent` near mux servers + central aggregator.
- Multi-agent coordination helpers.

## Why this phase exists
The core system can ship without this. These features are justified only when they deliver measurable wins:
- latency, correctness (fewer gaps), or usability.

## Deliverables
- Vendored client prototype + benchmarks vs CLI.
- If ROI confirmed: ship `--features vendored` with strict version checks.
- Distributed protocol design + first implementation.

## Acceptance criteria
- Benchmarks show clear advantage for vendored/streaming modes.
- Distributed mode can ingest deltas from at least one remote domain without stalling the central watcher.



## Success Criteria
- Advanced capabilities (vendoring, streaming, distributed mode, coordination) remain optional and do not compromise core safety.
- Each advanced feature has clear boundaries, tests, and safe defaults.


## Testing
- Performance ROI validation:
  - Benchmarks must prove vendored/streaming modes are meaningfully better than CLI polling for target workloads.

- Safety/regression:
  - Optional features must compile-gate cleanly and have their own unit/integration tests.

- E2E (when distributed mode is implemented):
  - Distributed E2E tests must validate persistence, ordering, and secret-safe artifacts (`wa-nu4.4.3.5`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.4.1","title":"[EPIC] Selective WezTerm vendoring (feature vendored) + version compatibility","description":"# [EPIC] Selective WezTerm vendoring

## Mission
Unlock capabilities not available via `wezterm cli`, while preserving a CLI-first default:
- zero-copy scrollback access
- real-time output streaming (enables true subscription vs polling)

## Default posture
- CLI-first remains the foundation.
- Vendoring is an **optional acceleration lane** behind `--features vendored`.

## Key risks
- Upstream API churn
- Version compatibility problems
- Larger binary / more complex build

## Definition of done
- `--features vendored` builds successfully.
- wa can detect local WezTerm version compatibility and surface it in `wa doctor`.
- Unified client picks best available backend (vendored when compatible, otherwise CLI).


## Success Criteria
- Vendored WezTerm components are minimal, clearly version-pinned, and do not break upstream compatibility expectations.
- CI validates vendor build and ensures core features still work without vendoring enabled.


## Testing
- Feature-gate tests:
  - Ensure `--features vendored` builds and unit tests run, and `--no-default-features` (or default) still works without pulling vendored deps.

- Compatibility tests:
  - Fixture-based tests for version/commit matching logic (supported/unsupported versions).
  - `wa doctor` should surface compatibility deterministically.

- RPC smoke tests:
  - Basic mux RPC framing tests with fixtures so failures are diagnosable without a live WezTerm.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.4.1.1","title":"Vendored feature scaffolding: add wezterm crates + compile behind feature flag","description":"# Task: Vendored feature scaffolding

## Goal
Add the minimal dependency + feature wiring to compile vendored WezTerm crates behind `--features vendored`.

## Deliverables
- Cargo feature `vendored`.
- Optional dependencies on the selected WezTerm crates (selective, not full fork).
- A build that succeeds in both modes:
  - default (CLI-only)
  - vendored

## Testing
- Build/test matrix:
  - `cargo check --all-targets` (default)
  - `cargo check --all-targets --features vendored`
  - ideally `cargo test --features vendored` once minimal unit tests exist

- Ensure feature gating is strict:
  - enabling `vendored` must not pull heavy deps into default builds

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo build` works without vendored deps.
- `cargo build --features vendored` works and does not affect default behavior.
